title: Removing and installing the SRS unit
section: Driver assistance SRS airbags seat belts

blocks.text: Removing and installing the SRS unit

items: Proceed with the work while observing work precautions. ​
Disconnect the cable from the minus (-) terminal of the 12V battery and wait at least 3 minutes before starting work.

blocks.text: When checking the manufacturer name of the SRS unit, use HDS to check the ECU manufacturer displayed in the data list.
Disconnect the coupler (A). Remove the Torx bolt (B) using the Torx bit T30 and remove the SRS unit (C).

items: Do not subject the SRS unit and its vicinity to any strong impact (not for removal and attachment using an impact wrench).

blocks.text: Installation is the reverse of the removal procedure.

items: Torx bolts shall be replaced with new ones at the time of installation and tightened to the specified torque.
The center lower cover must be installed after confirming the operation of the SRS warning light.
For TRW SRS units, check to see if the unit's program is up-to-date. If the program is not up-to-date, Update SRS unit.

blocks.text: Connect the cable to the (-) terminal of the 12V battery, turn the ignition to the ON mode, and check that the SRS warning light comes on for 6 seconds and then goes off.
